task_completion.description: Navigate to the OAuth applications administration page in OpenProject.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenProject administration interface, specifically the section for managing OAuth applications or creating a new OAuth application.

task_completion.description: Enter the required application name and Redirect URI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a form with 'Jenkins CI Pipeline' entered in the Name field and 'https://jenkins.intranet.example.com/oauth/callback' entered in the Redirect URI field.

task_completion.description: Select the required scopes and mark the application as Confidential.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'api_v3' checkbox selected (and 'openid' if available), and the 'Confidential' checkbox or toggle is enabled/checked.

task_completion.description: Successfully create the application and view the generated credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a success message or a summary page for the newly created 'Jenkins CI Pipeline' application, displaying a generated Client ID and Client Secret.

task_completion.description: Save the Client ID and Client Secret to the specified JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a terminal or text editor saving a file to '/home/ga/jenkins_credentials.json' containing the JSON format: {"client_id": "<actual_id>", "client_secret": "<actual_secret>"}.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenProject web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a real web browser navigating the OpenProject application, not a mock-up, text-based browser, or API testing tool.

integrity.description: The credentials saved in the JSON file match the ones generated by OpenProject.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Client ID and Client Secret visible in the JSON file exactly match the alphanumeric strings displayed on the OpenProject application success screen, rather than placeholder text.

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI to create the application, not backend database commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent using terminal commands (like psql or mysql) to directly insert the OAuth application record into the database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target OAuth application name must be exactly 'Jenkins CI Pipeline'. The target redirect URI must be exactly 'https://jenkins.intranet.example.com/oauth/callback'. The credentials must be saved to the exact file path '/home/ga/jenkins_credentials.json'.
